compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why

Phase 2a swapped the CI Module-size gate to an absolute cap + admin-locked limit that reads NEITHER the allowlist nor the ceilings file. Those two files are now orphaned (empty of module entries, read by nothing). Delete them and prune the docs that describe the retired escape hatch. This must land AFTER Phase 2a (5f9d, now merged) so main's gate has already stopped reading the files.

Also fixes THREE stale comments left by the reconcile split (ticket 3c7f): they still say route_inbound_probe lives in / stays in reconcile.py, but it moved to reconcile_helpers.py (re-exported through reconcile.py). Closing 3c7f is blocked on the first two; the third was found in review.

## What

1. `git rm` `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt` and `.github/module-size-ceilings.txt`.
2. `tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py`: add a test asserting the allowlist + ceilings files no longer exist.
3. `docs/architecture.md`: delete the '### Current offenders (> 800 LOC) and planned remedy' section entirely (its table now holds only a stale `attest.py` row); update the '## Module-size policy' prose to describe the ABSOLUTE 800 cap (no allowlist), the single-source `.github/module-size-limit.txt`, and that changing the limit requires an admin to override the CI gate.
4. `AGENTS.md`: update the module-size policy paragraph — remove the `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt` reference; state the cap is absolute and the limit is admin-locked.
5. Incidental (unblocks 3c7f): update the THREE stale comments so they say route_inbound_probe now lives in `reconcile_helpers.py` and is re-exported through reconcile.py —
   - `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py` (~line 177, the `route_inbound_probe stays here` comment)
   - `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/run_differs.py` (~line 16, the `reconcile.py keeps` comment)
   - `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_inbound.py` (~line 279, the `probe execution lives in reconcile.route_inbound_probe` docstring)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt` and `.github/module-size-ceilings.txt` no longer exist (verify: `! test -e .github/module-size-allowlist.txt && ! test -e .github/module-size-ceilings.txt`)
- [ ] `tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py` asserts both files are absent and passes (verify: `pytest tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py -q`)
- [ ] The docs/architecture.md offenders table section is gone and no allowlist reference remains (verify: `! grep -q 'Current offenders' docs/architecture.md && ! grep -q 'module-size-allowlist' docs/architecture.md`)
- [ ] AGENTS.md no longer references the allowlist (verify: `! grep -q 'module-size-allowlist' AGENTS.md`)
- [ ] ALL THREE stale reconcile residency comments are replaced (verify: `! grep -q 'route_inbound_probe stays here' src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py && ! grep -q 'reconcile.py keeps' src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/run_differs.py && ! grep -q 'probe execution lives in reconcile.route_inbound_probe' src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_inbound.py`)
- [ ] The reconciler unit suite is green (the comment edits are non-functional; verify: `pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py -q`)

## Notes
Mechanical deletion + doc/comment edits; no runtime code logic changes. E5: the contract test (updated) is the oracle for the deletion; the doc/comment edits are verified by the greps in the ACs. Lands via the normal Gerrit flow (Phase 2a's new gate does not read the deleted files, so their removal is green).

file_impact.reason: prune offenders table + update policy prose

compiled_state.title: Phase 2b: delete the drained allowlist/ceilings files + prune module-size docs
